name: MCP Hub for Neovim
what_it_does: MCP Hub is a Neovim plugin that acts as an MCP client, enabling developers to connect and manage MCP servers directly within their editor for use with AI coding assistant plugins like Avante.nvim and CodeCompanion.nvim.
use_cases: Integrating GitHub, Figma, and other external APIs with AI coding assistants inside Neovim
Managing multiple local and remote MCP servers across different projects with project-local configs
Running hot-reload development workflows for MCP server authors using Neovim
not_for: Non-Neovim editors or IDEs (VS Code, JetBrains, etc.)
Headless or server-side agent deployments without a Neovim instance
Users who do not already use Neovim-based AI plugins
best_when: You are a Neovim user who relies on Avante.nvim, CodeCompanion.nvim, or CopilotChat.nvim and want seamless MCP server management without leaving the editor.
avoid_when: Your team does not use Neovim, or you need a centralized multi-user MCP gateway rather than a per-developer editor plugin.

auth.notes: Supports OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for remote servers and custom HTTP header auth for API key-based services. Environment variable expansion in config.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error handling is editor-native; quality depends on Neovim notification system.
known_agent_gotchas: Designed for interactive Neovim sessions; not suitable for headless agent pipelines
Project-local configs must be manually set up per workspace
Multi-instance sync requires all Neovim instances to share the same hub daemon
